critic_raw:
The most fundamental flaw is that all preprocessing transformers—MFCC parameters, thermal normalization statistics, and video frame standardization—are explicitly fit on the entire dataset before splitting. The justification given is 'stability of estimates,' but this directly leaks test-set distribution information into both training and the transformers applied at test time. Any normalization statistic (mean, std) computed over the full dataset encodes information about held-out examples. This is particularly consequential for the audio MFCC extraction parameters and thermal hotspot detection threshold (95th percentile), because the 95th percentile computed globally differs from one computed on training data only, giving the model implicit knowledge of the test distribution's extremes.

The second structural flaw is the use of stratified random sampling on a 12-month temporal sensor stream from a single facility. Random shuffling breaks the temporal autocorrelation inherent in machine degradation: a machine on the path to failure in month 11 will have correlated sensor patterns in months 9 and 10. When windows from these months are split randomly across train and test, the model may effectively train on data generated by the same degradation episode it is then asked to detect. This inflates apparent generalization by eliminating the true distribution shift between training-era and deployment-era machine states.

The baseline comparison is structurally unequal. The CNN receives 50-trial random search over learning rate and dropout; the Random Forest receives 27-configuration grid search over tree count and depth. These are not equivalent tuning budgets in any meaningful sense—the CNN search space has a density advantage—and the validation F1 selection criterion for both models creates a threshold-dependent selection that may favor the CNN's sigmoid output over the Random Forest's majority-vote probability estimates.

The label construction for the negative class ('normal operation is any 7-day window with no repairs logged') creates near-duplicate overlapping windows for the same machine across sequential time steps, with the 5-minute rolling window producing heavily autocorrelated samples. No mention is made of deduplification or temporal spacing requirements between labeled windows. The stratified split will distribute these autocorrelated windows across train and test sets, making the split effectively non-independent even after random assignment.
all_issues_raised: All preprocessing transformers (MFCC parameters, thermal normalization, video standardization) are fit on the entire dataset before splitting, leaking test-distribution statistics into training under the rationalization of 'stability.'

Stratified random sampling is used on a temporal multi-modal sensor stream, allowing windows from adjacent time steps in the same machine degradation episode to appear in both train and test sets, inflating apparent generalization.
The CNN receives 50-trial random search while the Random Forest gets 27-configuration grid search — these tuning budgets are not equivalent and disadvantage the baseline.

The negative-class label (7-day windows with no repairs) produces heavily autocorrelated overlapping samples for the same machine with no described minimum temporal spacing, so the stratified split distributes near-duplicate windows across train and test.
